title: Nursery Rhyme. CLXVI. Songs.
content:
There was a jolly miller
            Lived on the river Dee:
        He worked and sung from morn till night,
            No lark so blithe as he,
        And this the burden of his song
            For ever used to be - 
        I jump mejerrime jee!
            I care for nobody - no! not I,
        Since nobody cares for me.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CLXVI. Songs."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
